steps.title: Eliminate the variable
steps.content:
From the second equation, solve for $y$ in terms of $x$:

$$-x+y=1$$
$$y=x+1$$

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content:
Substitute $y=x+1$ into the first equation:

$$x^2+(x+1)=21$$
$$x^2+x-20=0$$
$$(x+5)(x-4)=0$$

So the possible $x$-values are $x=-5$ and $x=4$.

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content: Only one of those possible values appears in the answer choices.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the two equations and read the $x$-coordinate of either intersection that appears in the choices. The answer is A.
